verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 (beta-1,4-galactosyltransferase 1) is a type II single-pass TM glycosyltransferase whose dominant localization is the trans-Golgi, where it transfers galactose during N-/O-glycan synthesis. However, B4GALT1 has a well-documented secondary cell-surface pool, particularly on sperm (where it functions as a zona-pellucida receptor binding ZP3) and on somatic cells where surface GalT participates in cell-cell and cell-matrix adhesion. Two alternative translation start sites generate a longer isoform with an extended cytoplasmic tail that is preferentially trafficked to the PM. The surface pool retains the catalytic ectodomain facing extracellularly. This is canonical dual_localization — Golgi-dominant with a documented, mechanistically-defined PM minority pool via its own TM anchor.
